rulings.Case Title: JOSE CERON ET AL VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories; Alternatively Motion To Compel Further Responses; Request For Sanctions Against Jose Ceron, Monica Medina, Brian Rangel, Wendy Henriquez And Their Attorneys Of Record Ed Higginbotham And Jeffrey Workman
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 2, 2019, Line 2 - DEFENDANTs SALLY LIU and JOHN LIU's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories; Alternatively Motion To Compel Further Responses; Request For Sanctions Against Jose Ceron, Monica Medina, Brian Rangel, Wendy Henriquez And Their Attorneys Of Record Ed Higginbotham And Jeffrey Workman. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants acted without substantial justification for their failure to respond to the unanswered interrogatories. Defendants are to provide full and complete responses to all unanswered interrogatories without any objection, within fourteen (14) days of notice of entry of order. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,460.00 against Jose Ceron, Monica Medina, Brian Rangel, Wendy Henriquez, and their attorneys, Ed Higginbotham and Jeffrey Workman, and payment of sanctions must be made within thirty (30) days of notice of entry of order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(D302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TANESH NUTALL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, PAUL HENDERSON, BARBARA GARCIA, MARY IVAS'DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. (part 1 of 2) Defendants City and County of San Francisco, Paul Henderson, Barbara Garcia, and Mary Ivas's demurrer to the first, second, third, fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. The filing requirements of the Government Claims Act (Gov't Code §§ 905, 905.2, 945.4) apply to all of Plaintiff's state law claims. "Unless a specific exemption applies, '[a] suit for "money or damages" [within the meaning of Gov't Code § 945.4] includes all actions where the plaintiff is seeking monetary relief, regardless whether the action is found in 'tort, contract or some other theory.'" (Lozada v. City and County of San Francisco (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 1139, 1152.) Ms. Nutall's damages claims are not merely incidental or subsidiary to claims for injunctive and declaratory relief, but constitute "the primary relief sought in the action." (Id. at 1166.) Here, just as in Loehr v. Ventura County Community College Dist. (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1071, 1081-1082, Ms. Nutall is seeking recovery for damages for mental suffering, humiliation, emotional distress, loss of earnings, and punitive damages, and also seeks civil penalties and attorney fees. The exception from the claims presentation requirements for FEHA claims, because of the administrative process mandated before such claims may be filed in court, does not apply to Ms. Nutall's claims under Civ. Code §§ 51, 51.5, 51.7 and Gov't Code § 11135. (See Gatto v. County of Sonoma (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 744, 764 ["Exceptions to the filing requirement not specifically enumerated in the Government Claims Act have occasionally been allowed, but only where the claim is based on a statute or statutory scheme that includes a functionally equivalent claim process."] [holding that claim filing requirements applies to damage actions under Civ. Code §§ 51 and 52.1].) Ms. Nutall has failed to plead facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with the claim presentation requirement of Gov't Code § 900 et seq. (State of California v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234, 1239-1243.) Ms. Nutall has not alleged her claim was delivered to the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors or the City's Controller or that her claim was actually received by the statutory recipients as required under Gov't Code § 915(e)(1). The judicial doctrine of substantial compliance does not apply to misdirected claims because the statute expressly prescribes when a misdirected claim satisfies the presentation requirement. (DiCampli-Mintz v. County of Santa Clara (2012) 55 Cal.4th 983, 992-993.) Similarly, the application of waiver depends on whether the statutory recipients actually received the claim. (See id. at 988 n.5 ["because plaintiff's claim was not presented to or actually received by the board or a person designated by it, sections 910.8, 911 and 911.3 are not applicable."].) A party's oversight in failing to file a timely claim or an application to file a late claim cannot justify equitable relief. (See J.M. v. Huntington Beach Union High School Dist. (2017) 2 Cal.5th 648, (citing Lantzy v. Centex Homes (2003) 31 Cal.4th 363, 371.)(end of part 1 of 2)=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TANESH NUTALL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Paul Henderson, Barbara Garcia, Mary Ivas Demurrer To Complaint (Part 2 Of 2 )(For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2019, Line 4, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Paul Henderson, Barbara Garcia, Mary Ivas Demurrer To Complaint (Part 2 Of 2 )(For Tentative Ruling Only) Because the complaint filed with the City Human Rights Commission substantially complies in content with a government claim, Ms. Nutall is given leave to amend her complaint and allege, if she can do so in good faith, that it was actually received by the statutorily designated recipients. (Stockett v. Association of Cal. Water Agencies Joint Powers Ins. Authority (2004) 34 Cal.4th 441, 446.) The declaration by Mr. Rothschild does not establish that the Human Rights Commission complaint was not "actually received by the clerk, secretary, auditor, or board of the local public entity." (Gov't Code §915(1).) Defendants' demurrer to the seventh cause of action for violation of Gov't Code § 11135 is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not adequately alleged that the training program she was attending is a "program or activity that . . . receives any financial assistance from the state." (Gov't Code § 11135(a); see Kirola v. City and County of San Francisco (N.D. Cal. 2014) 74 F.Supp.3d 1187, 1266 [Gov't Code § 11135 did not apply to mobility-impaired pedestrian's class action against city alleging denial of meaningful access to city's programs, services, and activities, absent evidence that programs at issue were funded directly by state, or received any financial assistance from state], aff'd in part and rev'd in part on other grounds, 860 F.3d 1164 (9th Cir. 2017).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of 2 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA KUZMINER VS. THE NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2019, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ANNA KUZMINER's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Anna Kuzminer's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The motion is supported by California's strong policy of liberality in amendment to pleading. Defendant has not shown that Plaintiff unduly delayed in seeking leave to amend or that it will be prejudiced if the amendment is allowed. As the amendment will not affect two of the three original causes of action in the complaint, and Plaintiff is no longer pleading a cause of action for breach of a written contract, Defendant's motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication as to those causes of action may be heard as scheduled on June 7, 2019. Defendant's challenges to the new proposed causes of action as "futile" may be raised by demurrer or a further motion for summary adjudication/summary judgment. If Defendant seeks to preserve the existing trial date, it may apply ex parte for leave to file such a further motion on shortened time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO ET AL VS. 2001 THAI STICK INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2019, Line 7, PLAINTIFF WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO, SAKSEE ISANPAYU, ARUNROJ WIBOON, AKARAPHON SODSAENGSUK, JATTALEE CHALERNHINTHONG, WARITTHA RASANAKORN's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs' motion to strike the answer of Defendants 2001 Thai Stick, Inc. and Thai Stk, Inc. is granted without prejudice to Defendants filing a motion to set aside the defaults entered against them. On September 17, 2018, Defendants' defaults were entered. Such entry of default cut off Defendants' opportunity to file a responsive pleading. (Forbes v. Cameron Petroleums, Inc. (1978) 83 Cal.App.3d 257, 262 ["Since defendants' default had already been entered, they had no standing to file any responsive pleading without first obtaining relief from the default."]; see also Devlin v. Kearny Mesa AMC/Jeep/Renault, Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 381, 385 ["The entry of a default terminates a defendant's rights to take any further affirmative steps in the litigation until either its default is set aside or a default judgment is entered."].) It follows that the Clerk of the Court erred in allowing Defendants to file their answer on March 25, 2019, and the answer must be stricken. Defendants may file a motion for relief from default. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: OZ NATHAN VS. ENIGMA MPC, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss/Alt. Stay, The Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT ENIGMA MPC, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss/Alternatively to Stay, The Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens. Defendant Enigma MPC, Inc.'s motion to dismiss this action based on forum non conveniens is granted. Plaintiff Oz Nathan and Defendant Guy Zyskind are both Israeli residents. Although Enigma is a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business in California, both it and Defendant Can Kisagun have consented to jurisdiction in Israel for the purpose of resolving this dispute and to the tolling of any applicable statutes of limitation during the pendency of the action in California. Enigma has also agreed to produce any of its three California employees in Israel. Israel therefore is a suitable alternative forum. (See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 752 ["Defendants stipulated that they would submit to jurisdiction in Sweden or Norway, respectively, as well as to the tolling of the statute of limitations during the pendency of the actions in California. Thus, the courts of Sweden and Norway present suitable forums for trial of the actions."].) Further, the balance of public and private factors favors Israel. The key witnesses in this case (Plaintiff and Defendant Guy Zyskind) both reside in Israel, it appears that the case arises out of actions that took place in large part in Israel, that most potential third-party witnesses reside in Israel, and that the majority of key documents are likely to be located in Israel. Finally, any judgment in this action could be most efficiently enforced in Israel, where Mr. Zyskind lives and the majority of his personal assets are located. California has a limited interest in resolving a dispute between two Israeli citizens concerning the management of a Delaware corporation nominally resident in California but amenable to suit in Israel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
